sequence.speaker: Brad
text: Abel! Get over here, I need an extra pair of hands!
Brad was red-faced and sweating, scurrying between different parts of the fish boil assembly with a manic energy.

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: Just kidding. Stand back, actually. This part gets hot.
The kerosene was tossed. A flash of smoke rose from the drums.
In that moment, Abel caught a startling sight — some of the odd fish [i]twitched[/i]. He strained his vision to confirm what his subconscious had noticed, but the flames and heat made it impossible.
His rational brain decided to chalk it up to heat haze. But his nervous system remained rattled.
Then the townsfolk began chanting. A uniform, droning sound — following no key signature or progression Abel had ever heard. Occasional whoops rang out, shouted without rhyme or reason.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Is... is Gregorian chanting a town pastime at this event?
Abel felt a wave of ice wash over him. Eyes he couldn't quite locate bore into him from all directions.

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: Old tradition! Goes way back. You get used to it.
Brad said this without looking up from the drums, his hands moving with a practiced rhythm that felt almost ceremonial.
The fish was brought out. Despite the oddities, there was something primally fulfilling about eating the fish he had worked so hard to catch.
Thankfully, the strange fish was not served to his side of the table.
